[Thu 2026-04-23 12:51 PDT] Create a new file `session-replay.html` in the repo root - a standalone HTML page for a "CAPTCHA Session Replay Analyzer". This is part of the gif-captcha research project.

## What to build

A single self-contained HTML file (no external dependencies except linking shared.css) that lets users:

1. **Record** a CAPTCHA-solving session - presents a simulated GIF CAPTCHA challenge and records all mouse movements, clicks, scroll events, and keystrokes with timestamps
2. **Replay** recorded sessions with a visual overlay showing mouse trails, click points, and timing patterns
3. **Analyze** the session with autonomous behavioral classification:
   - Movement smoothness score (humans are jittery, bots are smooth)
   - Click precision analysis (timing between events)
   - Pause pattern detection (humans pause to think, bots don't)
   - Velocity profile (acceleration/deceleration patterns)
   - Overall Human vs Bot confidence score with explanation
4. **Compare** two sessions side-by-side
5. **Auto-detect mode** that continuously monitors and flags suspicious patterns in real-time during recording

## Design requirements

- Link `<link rel="stylesheet" href="shared.css">` for base styles
- Use the same dark theme as other pages (--bg: #0d1117 etc.)
- Use Canvas for mouse trail visualization and replay
- All data stays client-side (localStorage for saved sessions)
- Include 3 pre-loaded demo sessions: one clearly human, one clearly bot, one ambiguous
- Professional layout with header, main content area, footer

## Page structure

- **Header**: "CAPTCHA Session Replay Analyzer" with subtitle "Record, replay, and analyze CAPTCHA solving behavior"
- **Tab bar**: Record | Replay | Compare | Analysis
- **Record tab**: Canvas area showing a simulated CAPTCHA (grid of colored squares, user clicks to identify specific ones), recording indicator, start/stop buttons
- **Replay tab**: Session picker dropdown, Canvas replay with playback controls (play/pause/speed), overlay toggles (trails, heatmap, velocity colors)
- **Compare tab**: Two side-by-side replay canvases with sync playback
- **Analysis tab**: Detailed metrics for selected session - radar chart of behavioral dimensions, timeline charts, verdict with confidence
- **Footer**: Standard gif-captcha footer

## Canvas visualizations
- Mouse trails with velocity-based color coding (slow=blue, fast=red)
- Click point markers with timing annotations
- Heatmap overlay of mouse dwell areas
- Velocity profile sparkline

## Bot detection algorithm
Score these dimensions 0-100:
- **Smoothness**: Compute angle changes between consecutive moves. Humans have high variance, bots low.
- **Timing entropy**: Shannon entropy of inter-event intervals. Humans have high entropy, bots have low.
- **Pause patterns**: Count and analyze pauses >500ms. Humans pause, bots rarely do.
- **Velocity consistency**: StdDev of velocities. Humans are inconsistent, bots are consistent.
- **Path efficiency**: Ratio of direct distance to actual path length. Bots are more efficient.
- **Click accuracy**: Time from last move to click. Humans overshoot and correct.

Combine into a weighted score and display as "Human Confidence: XX%" with explanation.

## Technical notes
- Use vanilla JS, Canvas API, no frameworks
- The simulated CAPTCHA should be a 3x3 grid of colored cells, user must click cells matching a target color
- Record MouseEvent data: {x, y, timestamp, type}
- Export/import sessions as JSON
- Keep file under 40KB

After creating the file, verify syntax: `node -c session-replay.html` won't work for HTML, so just make sure the JS has no obvious issues. Run `node -e "const fs=require('fs'); const h=fs.readFileSync('session-replay.html','utf8'); console.log('Size:',h.length,'bytes'); console.log('Has canvas:',h.includes('canvas')); console.log('Has shared.css:',h.includes('shared.css'));"` to verify basic structure.

Then:
1. `git add session-replay.html`
2. `git commit -m "Add CAPTCHA Session Replay Analyzer - record, replay, and analyze CAPTCHA solving sessions with autonomous bot detection, behavioral fingerprinting, Canvas visualizations, and side-by-side comparison"`
3. `git push origin HEAD:main`

I’m inspecting the repo structure first so I can match the existing dark theme and footer pattern before creating `session-replay.html`. After that I’ll add the standalone page, run the requested verification command, and handle the git steps if the workspace permissions allow it.

`rg` isn’t installed here, so I’m switching to PowerShell searches and reading a couple of nearby HTML pages to reuse the repo’s existing page shell, footer, and styling conventions. After that I’ll draft the new page with a tighter implementation so it stays under the size target.

I’ve confirmed the repo pattern: these pages link `shared.css`, keep page-specific styles inline, and use a simple `footer.footer` line tied back to `index.html`. I’m checking `shared.css` next so the new page inherits the same palette and spacing without duplicating base theme rules.

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
    <meta http-equiv="Content-Security-Policy" content="default-src 'none'; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; script-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; img-src https: data:; frame-ancestors 'none'; base-uri 'self'; form-action 'none';">
    <meta name="referrer" content="no-referrer">
    <title>GIF CAPTCHA — Research Analysis</title>
    <link rel="stylesheet" href="shared.css">
    <style>
        /* analysis.html — page-specific styles (base styles in shared.css) */
        .container { max-width: 960px; }

        h2 {
            font-size: 1.4rem;
            margin: 2.5rem 0 1rem;
            color: var(--accent);
            display: flex;
            align-items: center;
            gap: 0.5rem;
        }
        h2:first-child { margin-top: 0; }

        /* Summary cards */
        .summary-grid {
            display: grid;
            grid-template-columns: repeat(4, 1fr);
            gap: 1rem;
            margin-bottom: 2rem;
        }
        .summary-card {
            background: var(--surface);
            border: 1px solid var(--border);
            border-radius: 10px;
            padding: 1.25rem;
            text-align: center;
        }
        .summary-card .card-value {
            font-size: 2rem;
            font-weight: 800;
            line-height: 1.2;
        }
        .summary-card .card-label {
            font-size: 0.75rem;
            color: var(--muted);
            text-transform: uppercase;
            letter-spacing: 0.05em;
            margin-top: 0.3rem;
        }

        /* Filter tabs */
        .filter-tabs {
            display: flex;
            gap: 0.5rem;
            margin-bottom: 1.5rem;
            flex-wrap: wrap;
        }
        .filter-tab {
            padding: 0.4rem 1rem;
            border-radius: 20px;
            border: 1px solid var(--border);
            background: var(--surface);
            color: var(--muted);
            font-size: 0.8rem;
            cursor: pointer;
            transition: all 0.2s;
            font-family: inherit;
        }
        .filter-tab:hover { border-color: var(--accent); color: var(--text); }
        .filter-tab.active {
            background: var(--accent);
            color: #fff;
            border-color: var(--accent);
        }

        /* Canvas for charts */
        .chart-container {
            background: var(--surface);
            border: 1px solid var(--border);
            border-radius: 12px;
            padding: 1.5rem;
            margin-bottom: 1.5rem;
            position: relative;
        }
        .chart-container h3 {
            font-size: 1rem;
            margin-bottom: 1rem;
            color: var(--text);
        }
        .chart-container .chart-subtitle {
            font-size: 0.8rem;
            color: var(--muted);
            margin-top: -0.6rem;
            margin-bottom: 1rem;
        }
        canvas { display: block; width: 100%; }

        /* two-col layout — see shared.css */

        /* Detailed breakdown cards */
        .gif-analysis-card {
            background: var(--surface);
            border: 1px solid var(--border);
            border-radius: 10px;
            overflow: hidden;
            margin-bottom: 1rem;
            transition: border-color 0.2s;
        }
        .gif-analysis-card:hover { border-color: var(--accent); }
        .gif-analysis-card .card-header {
            padding: 1rem 1.25rem;
            display: flex;
            justify-content: space-between;
            align-items: center;
            cursor: pointer;
            user-select: none;
        }
        .gif-analysis-card .card-header .title-group {
            display: flex;
            align-items: center;
            gap: 0.6rem;
        }
        .gif-analysis-card .card-header .gif-number {
            width: 28px;
            height: 28px;
            border-radius: 50%;
            background: var(--accent);
            color: #fff;
            display: flex;
            align-items: center;
            justify-content: center;
            font-size: 0.8rem;
            font-weight: 700;
            flex-shrink: 0;
        }
        .gif-analysis-card .card-header .gif-title {
            font-weight: 600;
            font-size: 0.95rem;
        }
        .gif-analysis-card .card-header .expand-icon {
            color: var(--muted);
            transition: transform 0.2s;
            font-size: 0.8rem;
        }
        .gif-analysis-card.expanded .card-header .expand-icon { transform: rotate(180deg); }
        .gif-analysis-card .card-body {
            display: none;
            padding: 0 1.25rem 1.25rem;
            border-top: 1px solid var(--border);
        }
        .gif-analysis-card.expanded .card-body { display: block; }

        /* Tags (extends shared.css .tag with margin-bottom) */
        .tag { margin-bottom: 0.3rem; }

        /* Difficulty meter */
        .difficulty-meter {
            display: flex;
            align-items: center;
            gap: 0.5rem;
            margin: 0.8rem 0;
        }
        .difficulty-meter .meter-label {
            font-size: 0.75rem;
            color: var(--muted);
            width: 60px;
            flex-shrink: 0;
        }
        .difficulty-bar-bg {
            flex: 1;
            height: 6px;
            background: var(--border);
            border-radius: 3px;
            overflow: hidden;
        }
        .difficulty-bar-fill {
            height: 100%;
            border-radius: 3px;
            transition: width 0.6s ease;
        }
